Q30.r: The scheme contains no legally-binding or scheme-guideline-mandated obligation to replenish, restore, maintain, or sustain a commons resource. The 3-year survival monitoring requirement (78% target) and the maintenance allowance (₹500/tree/year × 3 years) are incentive mechanisms applied to private tree survival on private land — they are not replenishment obligations for a commons resource. The scheme's cumulative plantation target (42 lakh trees by 2026) is a state green-cover output goal, not a commons regeneration mandate. Official Notification No. 6-CMP/2015/CGN specifies no afforestation ratio on commons, no recharge structure mandate, no rotational grazing protocol, no seed-bank renewal obligation, no knowledge-transmission component — none of the qualifying replenishment/continuity rules per the SOC framework apply. No consequence mechanism for non-compliance with a replenishment obligation exists because no replenishment obligation exists. AUTOMATIC T4 FAIL triggered.
Q30.src: Official Notification No. 6-CMP/2015/CGN (2024 update — no commons replenishment clause found); Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q31.r: T1.1 (carried from t1_output.json) = 'C' (private land). Track selection rule: T1.1 ≠ E → track = RhoRatio. Q32 (ρ Ratio) is answered; Q33 (Vitality Index) is SKIP.

Q32.calc: ρ = Annual extraction enabled by scheme ÷ Annual replenishment mandated by scheme. Extraction: The scheme mandates tree planting on private land with no commons harvest component; no extraction from commons is enabled or tracked. Extraction from scheme activity = 0 units/year. Replenishment: Plantation survival = 3.5 lakh trees planted × 78% survival rate = 2.73 lakh trees surviving per year (Outcome Budget 2025-26). ρ = 0 ÷ 2.73 lakh = 0.00. NOTE: Concept is structurally inapplicable — there is no commons resource from which extraction occurs. The ρ ratio is computed using the only available data (plantation survival as a proxy for private-land green cover replenishment), yielding ρ = 0.00 technically within PASS threshold (≤1.2). This calculation is analytically vacuous: a ρ of 0 does not reflect a sustainable commons — it reflects the absence of any commons resource to extract from. Marked Estimated per T1.5 = No.
Q32.interp: PASS — ρ = 0.00 ≤ 1.2 (technical/formal PASS). However, this result is structurally meaningless: the scheme operates on private land with no commons resource extraction. C1_T4.1 = FAIL overrides this result — T4a partial PASS requires both C1_T4.1 = PASS AND ρ ≤ 1.2. Since C1_T4.1 = FAIL (Q30 = No), T4a partial result is FAIL regardless of ρ.
Q32.src: Outcome Budget 2025-26 (3.5 lakh trees planted, 78% survival); Official Notification No. 6-CMP/2015/CGN (no commons harvest mandate); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25
docs: Outcome Budget 2025-26 — trees planted (3.5 lakh) and survival rate (78%) data
Official Notification No. 6-CMP/2015/CGN — no commons extraction clause
CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — plantation records verification

Q33.calc: SKIP — track = RhoRatio (T1.1 = C ≠ E); Vitality Index does not apply

carry_for_t4b.C2_rho_or_VI: PASS (formal, vacuous — ρ = 0.00 ≤ 1.2; but concept inapplicable to private land scheme; moot given C1_T4.1 = FAIL)
